ddls:
CREATE TABLE "replicant_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each Replicant version */,
  "version_name" TEXT /* Name or version number of Replicant */,
  "version_code" TEXT /* Unique code or identifier for the version */,
  "base_system" TEXT /* Base system used (e.g., AOSP, CyanogenMod) */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date the version was released */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the version is currently active */,
  "security_patch_level" TEXT /* The level of the latest security patch included in the version */,
  "download_link" TEXT /* A link to download the version */,
  "changelog" TEXT /* A detailed changelog for the version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "supported_devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each supported device */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* ID of the Replicant version */,
  "device_model" TEXT /* Model of the device */,
  "device_name" TEXT /* Name of the device */,
  "device_brand" TEXT /* Brand of the device */,
  "device_architecture" TEXT /* Architecture of the device */,
  "device_cpu" TEXT /* CPU model of the device */,
  "device_memory" TEXT /* Amount of RAM in the device */,
  "device_storage" TEXT /* Storage capacity of the device */,
  "device_screen_resolution" TEXT /* Screen resolution of the device */,
  "is_supported" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the device is supported */,
  "special_notes" TEXT /* Any special notes or comments about device support */,
  "last_tested_date" TEXT /* Date the device was last tested with the version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_supported_devices_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES replicant_versions ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "source_mirrors" (
  "mirror_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each source mirror */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* ID of the Replicant version */,
  "mirror_url" TEXT /* URL of the source mirror */,
  "is_mirrored" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the mirror is active */,
  "mirror_type" TEXT /* Type of the mirror (e.g., AOSP, CyanogenMod) */,
  "mirror_location" TEXT /* Geographical location of the mirror */,
  "mirror_bandwidth" TEXT /* Bandwidth of the mirror */,
  "mirror_status" TEXT /* Status of the mirror */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mirror_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_source_mirrors_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES replicant_versions ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "releases" (
  "release_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each release */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* ID of the Replicant version */,
  "release_name" TEXT /* Name or number of the release */,
  "release_type" TEXT /* Type of release (e.g., stable, beta, alpha) */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date the release was made */,
  "release_notes" TEXT /* Notes or comments about the release */,
  "build_number" TEXT /* Build number of the release */,
  "download_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the release has been downloaded */,
  "md5_hash" TEXT /* MD5 hash of the release file for verification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("release_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_releases_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES replicant_versions ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "comments" (
  "comment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comment */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* ID of the Replicant version */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the comment */,
  "comment_text" TEXT /* Text of the comment or note */,
  "comment_type" TEXT /* Type of comment (e.g., technical, feedback, general) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date the comment was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date the comment was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_comments_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_comments_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES replicant_versions ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, developer, contributor) */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the user is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date the user account was created */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date the user last logged in */,
  "last_activity" TEXT /* Date of the user's last activity */,
  "avatar_url" TEXT /* URL of the user's profile picture */,
  "bio" TEXT /* A brief bio of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "device_features" (
  "feature_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device feature */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the supported device */,
  "feature_name" TEXT /* Name of the feature */,
  "support_level" TEXT /* Support level (e.g., full, partial, none) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the feature and its support */,
  "feature_category" TEXT /* Category of the feature (e.g., hardware, software) */,
  "feature_status" TEXT /* Current status of the feature (e.g., implemented, in progress, planned) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feature_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_device_features_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES supported_devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "version_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each historical entry */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* ID of the Replicant version */,
  "change_type" TEXT /* Type of change (e.g., update, deprecation) */,
  "change_details" TEXT /* Details of the change */,
  "changed_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the change */,
  "changed_at" TEXT /* Date the change was made */,
  "version_code" TEXT /* Unique code or identifier for the version */,
  "version_security_patch_level" TEXT /* Security patch level at the time of the change */,
  "version_download_link" TEXT /* Download link at the time of the change */,
  "version_changelog" TEXT /* Changelog at the time of the change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_version_history_changed_by FOREIGN KEY ("changed_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_version_history_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES replicant_versions ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "version_dependencies" (
  "dependency_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dependency */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* ID of the Replicant version */,
  "dependent_version_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dependent Replicant version */,
  "dependency_type" TEXT /* Type of dependency (e.g., required, optional) */,
  "dependency_reason" TEXT /* Reason for the dependency */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dependency_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_version_dependencies_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES replicant_versions ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_version_dependencies_dependent_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("dependent_version_id") REFERENCES replicant_versions ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_contributions" (
  "contribution_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each contribution */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* ID of the Replicant version */,
  "contribution_type" TEXT /* Type of contribution (e.g., code, documentation, testing) */,
  "contribution_details" TEXT /* Details of the contribution */,
  "contribution_date" TEXT /* Date the contribution was made */,
  "contribution_status" TEXT /* Status of the contribution (e.g., accepted, rejected, pending) */,
  "contribution_reviewer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who reviewed the contribution */,
  "contribution_review_date" TEXT /* Date the contribution was reviewed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("contribution_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_contributions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_contributions_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES replicant_versions ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_contributions_contribution_reviewer_id FOREIGN KEY ("contribution_reviewer_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
